PARTIES: 
This Agreement is made between: 
(1) 
Bootlegger Franchise Company Proprietary Limited, a company registered in accordance with the laws 
of South Africa under registration number [2017/428048/07] (Franchisor); 
(2) 
…………………………………………………………………………………., a company registered in accordance 
with the laws of South Africa  under registration number ……………………………………….. (Franchisee). 
WHEREAS 
A. 
The Franchisor has developed a specialised business that operates specialised restaurants under the 
name and style of Bootlegger Coffee Company. 
B. 
The Franchisee desires to establish such a business by using the methods and know-how of the Franchisor. 
C. 
The Franchisor hereby licenses such use of the Business System to the Franchisee subject to the terms and 
conditions of this Agreement. 
IT IS AGREED AS FOLLOWS: 
1. 
DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION 
 
Definitions 
For the purposes of this Agreement and the preamble above, unless the context requires otherwise: 
 
Act means the Consumer Protection Act 68 of 2008, the regulations and any amendments thereto; 
 
Agreement means this agreement, together with all annexures hereto; 
 
Associates means directors, shareholders, members, trustees or partners of the Franchisee; 
 
Business System means the system of operating a specialised restaurant business known as 
Bootlegger; 
 
Commencement Date means the date stipulated in Annexure A; 
 
Copyright means all rights of copyright whether existing now or in the future, including those rights 
in and to: 
1.1.6.1 
the Operations Manual; 
1.1.6.2 
the fixation of the Know-How, logos and procedures, whether incorporated in the Operations 
Manual or in any other documentation; and 
1.1.6.3 
other documentation and materials pertaining to the Business System and the Franchise System, 
including, without limitation, any software program;

Effective Date means the date of the last Party signing the Agreement; 
 
Franchised Business means a specialised restaurant business known as Bootlegger Coffee 
Company, to be operated by the Franchisee under licence from the Franchisor in accordance 
with the Business System and using the Intellectual Property; 
 
Franchise System means the entire franchise operation developed and operated by the 
Franchisor, including all Franchised Businesses operating in accordance with the Business System; 
 
First Renewal Period means a period of 5 (five) years from the expiry of this Agreement; 
 
Goodwill means the goodwill arising out of the use of the Business System and the Intellectual 
Property of the Franchisor; 
 
Intellectual Property includes, without limitation: 
1.1.13.1 
the Know-How including loyalty programmes; 
1.1.13.2 
the Copyright; 
1.1.13.3 
the Goodwill; 
1.1.13.4 
the Trade Dress; 
1.1.13.5 
the Trade Marks;  and 
1.1.13.6 
the Trade Secrets. 
 
Items means the specialised items used in the Business System as stipulated and as updated in the 
Operations Manual from time to time; 
 
Location means the designated area approved by the Franchisor for the Franchisee to lease the 
proposed Premises; 
 
Know-How includes, without limitation, all confidential, technical and commercial information 
relating to the operation of the Franchise System and Business System existing from time to time, 
including, without limitation, information contained in the Operations Manual or other documents, 
together with unrecorded information known to the Franchisor and to individuals who are office 
bearers, employees, partners, directors, members or shareholders of the Franchisor, as well as 
information known to Franchisees of the Franchise System; 
 
Marketing Fee means the fee payable by the Franchisee to the Franchisor for advertising and 
marketing of the Franchised Business, as stipulated in Annexure A; 
 
Marketing Fund means the advertising and marketing fund established by the Franchisor for the 
benefit of Franchisees in accordance with the requirements set out in the Act;

Nominated Operator means the operator, member, shareholder or partner of the Franchisee 
responsible for the overall management and control of the Franchised Business; 
 
Opening Date means the date of the Franchised Business opening for business; 
 
Operations Manual means the Franchisor's Operations and Procedures Manual, as amended from 
time to time and supplemented by further directions and regulations, written or otherwise, giving 
details of the Business System and Intellectual Property and imparting the Know-How;  
 
Parties means the Franchisee and Franchisor; 
 
Party means the Franchisee or the Franchisor as the context may indicate; 
 
Premises means the premises listed in Annexure A or such other premises as agreed by the 
Franchisor at which the Franchisee shall operate the Franchised Business; 
 
Products means the Items and other items listed in the Operations Manual to be sold at the

Termination Date means the 5th (fifth) anniversary of the Commencement Date;

the registered, as listed in Annexure C and as amended from time to time, and unregistered

1.1.32.2 
all current and/or future common law trade marks belonging to the Franchisor and relating to

Upfront License Fee means the fee payable by the Franchisee to the Franchisor for the right to

Interpretation 
 
In addition to the definitions in clause 1.1, unless the context requires otherwise: 
1.2.1.1 
the singular shall include the plural and vice versa; 
1.2.1.2 
a reference to any one gender, whether masculine, feminine or neuter, includes the other two. 
 
All the headings and sub-headings in this Agreement are for convenience only and are not to be 
taken into account for the purposes of interpreting it. 
2. 
GRANT OF LICENCE AND RENEWAL 
 
The Franchisor hereby grants to the Franchisee from the Effective Date a licence for the duration of 
this Agreement to operate the Franchised Business from the Premises and to: 
 
use the Intellectual Property together with the Business System strictly in accordance with the 
Operations Manual and subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement;  and 
 
the right to use the name “Bootlegger”, approved variations thereof and the logos related thereto. 
 
This Agreement and the licence granted in terms of clause 2.1 above will commence with effect from 
the Commencement Date, and terminate on the Termination Date unless otherwise terminated or 
renewed in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement. 
 
The Franchisee shall be entitled to renew this Agreement for the First Renewal Period by entering into 
the Franchisor’s standard franchise agreement in use in the Franchise System at that time, provided 
that: 
 
the Franchisee has complied with all the terms and conditions of this Agreement in that there are 
no unresolved breaches of this Agreement and that the Franchisee has not continually breached 
the terms of this Agreement; 
 
the Franchisee has given the Franchisor not less than 6 (six) and not more than 9 (nine) months’ 
written notice of its intention to extend this Agreement, prior to the termination of this Agreement;  
and 
 
the Franchisee has paid the Franchisor the standard Renewal Fee relevant at the time of giving the 
notice referred to in clause 2.3.2. 
 
In the event that the Franchisee elects to renew this Agreement the Franchisee undertakes to 
complete any renovations to the Premises reasonably required by the Franchisor, upon receiving 
written notice from the Franchisor at least 3 (three) months prior to the termination of this Agreement.  
All such renovations are to be completed prior to the renewal of this Agreement.   
 
This Agreement shall continue to be in full force and effect irrespective of the lapsing, invalidity or 
unenforceability of any registered or unregistered Intellectual Property.

3. 
LEASE 
 
The Franchisee undertakes to negotiate the lease agreement in respect of the Premises such that the 
Franchisor has a first right of refusal to take assignment of the lease agreement upon termination of 
this Agreement and/or the lease for any reason whatsoever.  
 
If the landlord refuses such right of first refusal, the Franchisee hereby consents to the Franchisor 
communicating directly with the landlord, at any time to negotiate and secure same. In the event 
that the lease agreement does not provide for such assignment, the Franchisor shall be entitled to 
withhold consent to the Franchisee being located at such premises. 
 
The Franchisee shall not enter into a lease agreement in respect of the Premises unless the Franchisor 
has approved same in writing and the Franchisor has provided the Franchisee with an initial design 
and development appraisal, which will provide a cost assessment of the fitting-out of the proposed 
premises. 
 
Notwithstanding clause 3.2 the Franchisee, upon written request by the Franchisor, agrees to assign or 
sub-lease the Premises to the Franchisor upon termination of this Agreement and to sign all 
documentation necessary to give effect to same. 
4. 
SELECTION OF PREMISES 
 
In the event that the Franchisee does not secure premises approved by the Franchisor within the 
Location, within 90 (ninety) days of the Signature Date, which period may be extended at the sole 
discretion of the Franchisor this Agreement shall lapse and the Franchisee will be refunded that portion 
of the payment made in terms of clause 5 less all costs incurred by the Franchisor in terms of clause 4.3. 
 
In the event that the Franchisee proposes premises to the Franchisor, the following information and 
research shall be required by the Franchisor from the Franchisee, which is to be set out in a written 
report to the Franchisor prior to a physical inspection of the proposed premises being conducted: 
 
a detailed location plan, with extensive photos of the site and the surrounding area; 
 
an analysis of the office district within a 10 (ten) minute walk of the proposed premises, the number 
of people working within that area, names of the major office tenants within close proximity to the 
proposed premises and major office tenants within close proximity which operate an in-house 
canteen/cafeteria;  
 
an analysis of how many businesses, substantially similar to the Franchised Business, are situated 
within close proximity to the proposed premises; 
 
an analysis of how many people work within close proximity of the proposed premises and an 
analysis of the location of parking bays in close proximity to the proposed premises; 
 
an analysis of other customer segments including tourists, residents and shoppers within close 
proximity to the proposed premises; 
 
an analysis of potential competition to the Franchised Business within close proximity to the 
proposed premises, setting out which businesses are potential competition, where such businesses 
DocuSign Envelope ID: B91ADC09-CA91-4454-ADB0-22E6783FF674


<!-- PAGE 8 START -->
 
 
 
page | 7 
Bootlegger_Franchise Agreement_2020_v2.1.docx 
are located, what such businesses offer,  how long such businesses have been in operation, and 
how successful such businesses are; 
 
a summary of the material terms of the lease agreement in respect of the proposed premises, 
including, but not limited to the following terms and conditions:  
4.2.7.1 
the rental amount; 
4.2.7.2 
escalation percentage;  
4.2.7.3 
duration; and 
4.2.7.4 
renewal options.   
 
an analysis of any other notable features of the proposed premises. 
 
The proposed premises will be evaluated by the Franchisor in considering the report and in the event 
that the Franchisor considers the proposed premises worthy of inspection, the Franchisor will notify the 
Franchisee of a time and date for an inspection to be held, within 14 (fourteen) days of receiving the 
report.   
 
Once such an inspection has been held the Franchisor shall consider all aspects including the 
commencement viability and shall notify the Franchisee of its decision. 
 
If the Premises are acceptable to the Franchisor it will assist the Franchisee in lease negotiations to 
secure the Premises. 
5. 
UPFRONT LICENSE FEE 
 
The Upfront License Fee shall be paid by the Franchisee to the Franchisor on Signature Date. 
 
The Upfront License Fee or any part thereof shall be returned to the Franchisee should the Agreement 
be terminated at any stage prior to the Commencement Date, and for whatever reason, subject to 
the Franchisor deducting any costs reasonably incurred by the Franchisor in respect of the negotiation 
and investigation of this Agreement with the Franchisee, including the visitation and assessment of any 
proposed premises. For the avoidance of doubt, this will include reasonable airfare, car-hire and 
accommodation costs incurred by the Franchisor in the process of visiting and assessing any proposed 
premises. 
6. 
MARKETING FUND 
 
The Franchisee shall contribute the Marketing Fee to the independent Marketing Fund in accordance 
with Annexure A hereto. 
 
The Franchisee shall pay the Marketing Fee by the 7th (seventh) day of each month. 
 
The Parties record that the Franchisor shall use the Marketing Fee towards promoting, advertising and 
marketing the Franchised Business and Franchise System locally and , if applicable, nationally. 
 
The Marketing Fee shall be paid to the Marketing Fund which shall be utilised to promote the Franchise 
System. 
DocuSign Envelope ID: B91ADC09-CA91-4454-ADB0-22E6783FF674


<!-- PAGE 9 START -->
 
 
 
page | 8 
Bootlegger_Franchise Agreement_2020_v2.1.docx 
 
The Franchisor shall manage the Marketing Fund in its sole discretion subject to consultation with the 
Franchisees as follows: 
 
an advertising and marketing forum will be created by the Franchisees and such elected 
Franchisees shall assist the Franchisor to promote and market the Franchised Business; 
 
within six months after the end of a financial year provide the Franchisee with a copy of a financial 
statement prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting practice and applicable 
legislation, which fairly reflects the marketing funds’ receipts and expenses for the preceding 
financial year, including amounts spent and the method of spending on advertising and/or 
marketing of franchisees and the Franchise Systems Products and services; 
 
in addition to the obligation in clause 6.5.2, the Franchisor shall ensure that: 
6.5.3.1 
the Franchisor shall every 3 (three) months, on request, furnish the Franchisee with financial 
management accounts relating to funds available to the franchisees, in the Marketing Fund; 
6.5.3.2 
the Marketing Fund contributions and monies in the fund shall not be spent on advertising and 
marketing of the Franchisors’ franchises for sale; 
6.5.3.3 
in the event that an audit is carried out on the Marketing Fund, a certificate of a registered 
auditor or accounting officer as the case may be, confirming that the Marketing Funds account 
has been audited and that the Marketing Funds financial statements are to the best of his or 
her knowledge a true reflection of the accounts of the Marketing Fund.  Where no audit has 
been carried out, a certificate must be issued by the accountant stating that the management 
accounts have been prepared and are correct to the best of the member’s knowledge; 
6.5.3.4 
a Franchisee is entitled to request a copy of the statement and certificate issued in terms of 
clause 6.5.3.3 and the Franchisor shall be obliged to provide the Franchisee with copies thereof 
within a reasonable period; 
6.5.3.5 
any marketing contributions paid to the Marketing Fund shall be deposited into a separate bank 
account and used only for the purposes of the fund; 
6.5.3.6 
the Franchisor shall provide the franchisees from time to time with details of any contributions 
by the Franchisor to the Marketing Fund, if any.  
7. 
FRANCHISE FEE 
 
The Franchisee shall pay the monthly Franchise Fee to the Franchisor in accordance with Annexure A. 
 
In respect of the Franchise Fee and/or any other fees payable at any time to the Franchisor, the 
Franchisor may require any information or documentation from the Franchisee reasonably required to 
enable the Franchisor to determine whether the Franchise Fee and/or any other fee has been 
correctly assessed and/or determined and/or calculated by the Franchisee, and such information or 
documentation shall be furnished promptly by the Franchisee, and in any event no later than 3 (three) 
business days from receiving written notice.  In addition, the Franchisor may require the certificate of 
an independent auditor certifying that any particular Franchise Fee and/or any other fee due has 
been correctly assessed and/or determined and/or calculated.  If any particular Franchise Fee and/or 
DocuSign Envelope ID: B91ADC09-CA91-4454-ADB0-22E6783FF674


<!-- PAGE 10 START -->
 
 
 
page | 9 
Bootlegger_Franchise Agreement_2020_v2.1.docx 
any other fee paid is found, by any such independent auditor, to differ by more than 5% (five percent) 
from such auditor’s assessment and/or determination and/or calculation, the Franchisee shall be liable 
for the costs incurred in the conducting of such audit.  In addition, the Franchisee shall pay to the 
Franchisor any deficit identified in the auditor’s assessment and/or determination and/or calculation.  
The provisions of this clause do not limit the Franchisor’s rights in terms of clause 17 in any way.  
 
Payment of the Franchise Fee shall be made by the 7th (seventh) day of the month, in respect of the 
amount owing for the previous month, by means of an electronic funds transfer or debit order and 
simultaneously with payment the Franchisee shall forward its standard declaration of Turnover to the 
Franchisor. 
 
The Franchisee shall pay for Products purchased from the Franchisor by electronic funds transfer within 
7 (seven) days of invoice from the Franchisor. 
 
Franchisor shall have the right, which Franchisee hereby gives to Franchisor, at any time and  without 
prior notice to Franchisee to communicate directly with  the  Franchisee’s auditors and/or accountants 
and to instruct such person to allow Franchisor access to inspect and audit or cause to be inspected 
and audited, the business records, bookkeeping and accounting records, sales and income tax 
records and returns and other records of the Franchised Business and the books and records of the 
company, close corporation or partnership which owns the Franchisee.   Franchisee shall fully co-
operate with representatives of Franchisor and/or independent accountants hired by Franchisor to 
conduct any such inspection or audit.  If any such inspection or audit discloses an understatement of 
the gross sales of the Franchised Business, the Franchisee shall pay to Franchisor, within fifteen (15) days 
after receipt of the inspection or audit report, the Franchise and Marketing Fees due on the amount 
of such understatement, plus interest at the rate prescribed herein from the date originally due until 
the date of payment.  Further, if such inspection or audit is made necessary by the failure of Franchisee 
to furnish reports, supporting records or other information as herein required, or to furnish such reports, 
records or information on a timely basis or if an understatement of gross sales for the period of any 
audit is determined by any such audit or inspection, Franchisee shall reimburse Franchisor with the 
costs of such audit or inspection, including, without invitation, the charges of any independent 
accountants and the travel expenses, accommodation and compensation of employees of 
Franchisor. 
8. 
SET-UP 
 
The Franchisor shall develop and install the Premises in terms set out in the Design and Costing 
Document, referred to in clause 10.6. 
 
The Franchisor shall provide pre-opening support, assistance and strategy for the duration of the first 
30 (thirty) days after the Opening Date, including only reasonable support and assistance at the 
Premises. 
 
The Design and Costing Document shall set out and provide the Franchisee with cost and details of 
the full turn-key development in terms of which the Franchisor shall hand over a completed Premises 
ready for beneficial occupation and fitted-out in accordance with the Franchisor’s specifications.

9. 
PAYMENTS 
 
Any amounts due in terms of this Agreement shall be paid in full by the Franchisee as stipulated and 
without any deduction or set-off whatsoever. 
 
The Franchisee will be liable for the payment of VAT, where applicable, on all the amounts due in 
terms of this Agreement. 
 
Interest shall be payable at the prime rate plus 2% (two percent) on any overdue amounts owing in 
terms of this Agreement. 
 
For the purposes of clause 9.3, prime rate means the prime rate of interest levied from time to time by 
the ABSA Bank Limited on unsecured overdrawn current accounts of its most favoured corporate 
customers in the private sector, as certified by any manager of that bank (whose authority and/or 
appointment and/or qualification it shall not be necessary to prove) calculated on the daily balance 
and capitalised monthly in arrears. 
 
If any amount is payable on a monthly basis, the Franchisee will be responsible for payment on a pro-
rata basis, should the Agreement either commence or be terminated during the course of a month. 
10. 
OBLIGATIONS OF THE FRANCHISOR 
 
The Franchisor shall, provided that payment of the Upfront License Fee referred to in clause 5 has been 
duly made: 
 
disclose the Business System to the Franchisee and make available the Intellectual Property 
licensed to the Franchisee in terms of this Agreement; and 
 
 ensure that the Upfront License Fee be utilised for all preliminary expenses and related costs of the 
Franchise. 
 
The Franchisor will assist and advise the Franchisee on the following matters relating to the 
establishment of the Franchised Business: 
 
the Trade Dress; 
 
the Premises; and 
 
the structure and method of operation of the Franchised Business as set out in the Operations 
Manual. 
 
The Franchisor shall provide the Franchisee with a list of approved suppliers of the Items, on a 
continuing basis, which may be the Franchisor or one of its affiliated entities.  All suppliers must be 
approved by the Franchisor to ensure quality, it being acknowledged that the quality of the Items is 
essential to the business. The Franchisor shall not unreasonably withhold its approval of any alternate 
supplier(s) suggested by the Franchisee provided that the Items supplied by such supplier/s are of the 
requisite quality as approved by the Franchisor. 
 
The Franchisor shall assist the Franchisee with standard contracts of employment. 
DocuSign Envelope ID: B91ADC09-CA91-4454-ADB0-22E6783FF674


<!-- PAGE 12 START -->
 
 
 
page | 11 
Bootlegger_Franchise Agreement_2020_v2.1.docx 
 
The Franchisor shall recommend labour consultants to the Franchisee in order to assist with labour 
relations. 
 
Within 30 (thirty) days of a lease agreement in respect of the Premises being concluded the Franchisor 
shall provide the Design and Costing Document to the Franchisee. 
11. 
TRAINING 
 
The Franchisor shall provide initial training to the Franchisee, which is included in the Upfront License 
Fee, and shall provide additional assistance as set out herein. 
 
The initial training of the Franchisee or its Nominated Operator will take place prior the 
Commencement Date at a store nominated by the Franchisor, which training will be held in Cape 
Town.  The duration of the initial training will be 60 (sixty) days unless otherwise determined by the 
Franchisor.  The Franchisee will be responsible for the payment of all expenses, including travel and 
subsistence expenses for the duration of the training period.  
 
The Franchisor will provide on-site training for the duration of the first 30 (thirty) days after the Opening 
Date. 
 
The Franchisor, in consultation with the Franchisee, will, from time to time, as necessary, determine 
which of the Franchisee's office bearers, employees, partners, directors, members or shareholders 
(including the Franchisee personally, if applicable) will undergo training in the Business System.  This 
training will take place at the first available training programme and whenever deemed necessary by 
the Franchisor, at a place chosen by the Franchisor.  The extent and duration of any training will be 10 
(ten) days unless otherwise determined by the Franchisor.  The Franchisee will be responsible for the 
payment of all  reasonable expenses, including travel expenses, and subsistence of a trainee as well 
as the trainee's salary payable during the training period. 
 
In addition to the training in the Business System provided for above, the Franchisor may also hold 
training seminars and conferences (the length, frequency and nature of which shall be determined 
by the Franchisor in its sole discretion, acting reasonably) catering specifically for Franchisees.  The 
Franchisee is obliged to attend such seminars, at the request of the Franchisor, one of which is 
compulsory.  The Franchisee will be responsible for the payment of all  reasonable expenses, including 
travel expenses, and subsistence of the Franchisee as well as the Franchisee’s salary payable during 
the period of the seminar. 
 
The Franchisor will disclose improvements, new programme material and themes, products and 
developments in the Business System including loyalty programmes to the Franchisee and will provide 
such further training to the Franchisee and other persons engaged in the conduct of the Franchised 
Business as the Franchisor considers necessary.  The Franchisee will be responsible for all the expenses 
of the persons undergoing such training including travelling expenses and subsistence as well as their 
salaries payable during the period of training.  
 
The Franchisor will assist the Franchisee in endeavouring to overcome problems which the Franchisee 
may experience in operating the Franchised Business, and for this purpose will make available to the 
Franchisee suitably qualified personnel as soon as is reasonably practical.  The Franchisee will be 
responsible for the expenses of a staff member in travelling from the office of the Franchisor to the

Franchisee's Premises as well as for the subsistence of the staff member for the duration of the staff 
member's attendance on the Franchisee including a pro rata share of the cost to company of such 
staff member. 
 
The Franchisor will furnish the Franchisee with a copy of all updates and amendments to the 
Operations Manual made by it from time to time. Where improvements and additions to the Business 
System involve improvements and additions to the Intellectual Property, these will be disclosed to the 
Franchisee and will form part of the Intellectual Property, use of which is licensed to the Franchisee in 
terms of this Agreement and will be included in the updated Operations Manual. 
 
The Franchisor shall furnish to the Franchisee's management a training program in the operation of the 
Franchised Business during such period as the Franchisor designates prior to the Commencement 
Date. The training program may be at the Franchisor's principal offices, and/or at the Franchised 
Business. The Franchised Business’ general manager and such other personnel as hereinbefore referred 
to must pursue and complete the Franchisor's training program to the sole, subjective satisfaction of 
the Franchisor.  The Franchise Business’ general manager and such other personnel as the Franchisor 
may require must be hired and in training at least sixty (60) days prior to the Commencement Date 
unless the Franchisor otherwise agrees. 
 
Subsequently hired general managers must pursue and complete the Franchisor's training program to 
the Franchisor's sole satisfaction. 
 
The Franchisor will furnish such training and any additional guidance and assistance required by the  
Franchisee  from  time to time at the Franchisor’s standard fees cost in providing such training, 
guidance and assistance. 
 
If, in the Franchisor's sole opinion, it is reasonably necessary, the Franchisor will furnish personnel to 
supervise the operation of the Franchised Business until such training is completed. Supervision of the 
operation of the Franchised Business during any such period shall be for and on behalf of the 
Franchisee, provided that the Franchisor shall only have a duty to utilise its best efforts and shall not be 
liable to the Franchisee or its owners for any debts, losses, or obligations incurred by the Franchised 
Business, or to any creditor of the Franchisee for any food products, materials, equipment, fixtures, 
furnishings, supplies or services purchased by the Franchised Business during such period. The 
Franchisee shall reimburse the Franchisor upon demand for all of the Franchisor's direct and indirect 
costs in connection with such supervision, including personnel costs, travel and living expenses. 
 
The  Franchisor  may,  at  its  election,  require  the Franchisee  to  replace  any  manager who in the 
Franchisor's reasonable determination does not meet the Franchisor's standard for knowledge and 
performance or who does not pursue or complete the Franchisor's training program to the Franchisor's 
sole satisfaction. The Franchisor may require Franchisee and/or previously trained and experienced 
managers to attend periodic refresher courses at locations designated by the Franchisor. 
 
The Franchisee shall be responsible for all travel and living expenses which he, his manager and other 
personnel incur in connection with initial training and any subsequent training program, guidance or 
assistance.

The Franchisor  shall  advise  the Franchisee  from  time  to  time  of  operating  problems  of  the 
Franchised Business disclosed by reports submitted to or inspections made by the Franchisor or by its 
representatives and shall furnish to Franchisee guidance in connection with: 
 
methods, standards and operating procedures used by the Franchised Business; 
 
preparation of food products and development of new products; 
 
 purchasing  approved  equipment,  furnishings,  fixtures,  signs,  food  products, beverages and 
supplies; 
 
 advertising and promotional programs;  
 
employee training; and 
 
administrative, bookkeeping, accounting and general operating and management procedures.  
 
Such guidance shall, at the discretion of the Franchisor, be furnished in the form of the Franchisor's 
Operating Manuals, other written materials and/or telephonic consultations or consultations at the 
offices of the Franchisor or at the Premises. 
 
The Franchisor will provide on loan to Franchisee during the term of this Agreement one copy of the 
Operating Manual. The Operating Manual shall contain mandatory and suggested specifications, 
standards and operating procedures prescribed from time to time by the Franchisor for the Franchised 
Business and information relative to other obligations of Franchisee hereunder and the operation of 
the Franchised Business. The Operating Manual may be modified by the Franchisor from time to time 
to reflect changes in the image, decor, design, format, appearance, methods, standards and 
specifications approved for Franchised Business.  The Franchisee may not at any time make any 
unauthorised use or disclosure of or copy any part of the Operating Manual and if the Franchisee does 
so it shall, without prejudice to the Franchisor's other legal remedies, be liable in respect of each such 
use, disclosure or copy, to pay the Franchisor a sum equal to the aggregate of the royalties due and/or 
paid by the Franchisee for the previous three completed months or R30 000,00 (Thirty Thousand Rand), 
whichever is the greater as pre-estimated damages. 
 
The Franchisor is not entitled to any undisclosed direct or indirect benefit or compensation from 
suppliers to its Franchisees unless the fact thereof is disclosed in writing with an explanation of how it 
will be applied. 
12. 
OBLIGATIONS OF THE FRANCHISEE 
 
The Franchisee will operate the Franchised Business strictly in accordance with the Business System and 
will ensure strict adherence to the Operations Manual, supplemented by any specifications and 
directions, written or otherwise, as may be laid down or given by the Franchisor from time to time. The 
Franchisee will have the right to use, and undertakes to use, the Intellectual Property in relation to the 
Franchised Business, provided that the Franchisee conducts the Franchised Business strictly in 
accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the Business System.

The Franchisee will be obliged to use the Items, or such similar items approved by the Franchisor (acting 
reasonably) as directed by the Franchisor from time to time, subject to the price of the Items being 
market-related and reasonable under the circumstances. 
 
The Franchisee will ensure that the Franchised Business conforms to other Franchised Businesses 
operated in accordance with the Business System.  The Franchisee acknowledges that such 
conformity is essential for the successful operation of the Franchised Business and the Franchise System, 
including other businesses operated in accordance with the Business System, as well as for the proper 
protection of the Intellectual Property, in particular the Trade Marks and the Goodwill.  The Franchisee 
will protect and promote the Goodwill associated with the Franchised Business and all Goodwill 
generated by the conduct of the Franchised Business will inure to the benefit of the Franchisor.  
 
The Franchisee acknowledges that conformity as far as recommended pricing is concerned is 
essential for the successful operation of the Franchised Business and especially the Franchise System, 
and the Franchisee therefore undertakes to comply as far as possible with the recommended pricing 
structure that may be stipulated by the Franchisor from time to time.  The Franchisee in any event 
undertakes not to charge prices that are higher than those mentioned in the recommended pricing 
structure without the Franchisor’s prior written consent.  Should the Franchisee elect to charge prices 
which are lower than those specified in the recommended pricing structure in the disclosure 
document or operations manual as amended from time to time, the Franchisee undertakes to obtain 
the prior written consent of the Franchisor, which consent will not be unreasonably withheld. 
 
The Franchisee will not commence the conduct of the Franchised Business until the persons referred 
to in clause 11 have undergone the initial training referred to therein.  The Franchisee will not permit 
any person to act or assist in the Franchised Business in any capacity whatsoever unless such person 
has undergone a course of training referred to in clauses 11.1, 11.4, 11.5 and/or 11.6 and has been 
approved as competent by the Franchisor. 
 
The Franchisee shall upon the commencement of the Franchised Business and from time to time 
thereafter engage all staff and other persons required for the effective operation of the Franchised 
Business in compliance with this Agreement.  The Franchisee will ensure that all staff members who are 
not required to be trained by the Franchisor in terms of this Agreement are nevertheless adequately 
trained in the Business System to the satisfaction of the Franchisor where it is necessary for the 
performance of their duties.  In the event that the Franchisee fails to ensure that all staff members, 
who are not required to be trained by the Franchisor in terms of this Agreement, are adequately 
trained in the Business System to the reasonable satisfaction of the Franchisor, the Franchisor may 
conduct its own training sessions that will be for the account of the Franchisee, provided that the 
Franchisor has given the Franchisee written notice setting out which staff members are inadequately 
trained and the Franchisee has failed to train such staff members adequately within 7 (seven) days of 
having received such notice.    
 
The Franchisee acknowledges that neither it nor its office bearers, employees, partners, directors, 
members or shareholders have any prior knowledge of the Business System or any aspect of the Know-
How or the Trade Secrets.  The Franchisee will not divulge or permit to be divulged to any person any 
aspect of the Business System, the Know-How or the Trade Secrets otherwise than for the purposes of 
this Agreement.  The Franchisor may by written notice to the Franchisee specify which of the 
DocuSign Envelope ID: B91ADC09-CA91-4454-ADB0-22E6783FF674

Franchisee's office bearers, employees, partners, directors, members or shareholders are required to 
sign a confidentiality agreement and no such person will be employed in the conduct of the 
Franchised Business until he or she has signed such a confidentiality undertaking which will be in a form 
stipulated by the Franchisor from time to time.  The Franchisee undertakes not to engage the services, 
for the purposes of this Agreement, of any person other than its employees. 
 
The Franchisee will not do, cause or permit anything to be done which may adversely affect the 
Intellectual Property or the Franchisor's rights in the Intellectual Property.  The Franchisee will forthwith 
bring to the attention of the Franchisor any infringement of the Intellectual Property that may come 
to its attention.  The Franchisee will give all reasonable assistance to the Franchisor to enable the latter 
to further or defend its rights in the Intellectual Property. 
 
The Franchisee acknowledges and confirms that the Franchisor is or will be the owner of all rights of 
whatever nature in the Intellectual Property including all additions, modifications, changes or updates 
to the Business System and the Intellectual Property, and that it has no claims of any nature to either 
the Business System or to any element of the Intellectual Property.   
 
The Franchisee shall not at any time attack or challenge the rights of the Franchisor to the Intellectual 
Property or induce or procure any other person to attack or challenge such rights, whether directly or 
indirectly. 
 
The Franchisee will not advertise or conduct promotional or marketing activities or allow such activities 
without the prior written approval of the Franchisor.  Ownership of Copyright in all material generated 
by advertising campaigns shall vest solely in the Franchisor and the Franchisee shall only use approved 
designers for its marketing materials. 
 
The Franchisee (including its office bearers, employees, partners, directors, members or shareholders, 
where applicable) will not engage directly or indirectly in any capacity in any other business venture 
which is in the nature of or related or similar to or tends to compete with the Franchised Business without 
the prior written consent of the Franchisor, including business ventures such as corporate events and 
workshops.   
 
In the event that the Franchisee wishes to arrange evening functions, workshops or events at the 
Premises, the Franchisee must obtain prior written approval from the Franchisor. The Franchisee must 
provide reasonable written details of the proposed event to the Franchisor, including the proposed, 
menu, quotation of costs associated with the event and reasons for the event.  
 
For the sake of clarity, the income generated, including venue hire, from such an event shall be 
included in the calculation of the turnover of the Franchised Business and shall be entered into the 
point of sale system as per normal sales generated by the Franchised Business.   
 
The Franchisee will comply in the conduct of the Franchised Business with all applicable laws, by-laws 
and regulations of any competent authority.   
 
The Franchisee will not induce other Franchisees’ employees to leave their employment and take up 
employment with the Franchisee.

The Franchisee will permit the Franchisor or its duly authorised representative at all reasonable times to 
carry out such inspections or investigations which the Franchisor may consider necessary for the 
purposes of ascertaining whether the provisions of this Agreement are being complied with. The 
Franchisee will ensure that it and its employees co-operate fully in such inspections or investigations.  
The Franchisor shall be responsible for the cost of such inspections or investigations (i.e. operational 
and business efficiency audits including store visits).  In the event that the Franchisee fails to take action 
on the points raised from these reports, the resultant time and effort made by the Franchisor or its 
appointed representative in order to correct these problems will be for the account of the Franchisee. 
 
In addition to operational and business efficiency audits the Franchisor will conduct hygiene audits.  
The Franchisee will comply and abide with the health and safety and hygiene rules at all times, as set 
out in clause 12.27, and any areas found unsatisfactory by such audits are to be remedied 
immediately by the Franchisee upon receipt of written notice from the Franchisor. 
 
The Franchisee hereby indemnifies the Franchisor and will keep it indemnified, for the duration of this 
Agreement and thereafter, against all claims of whatever nature, together with any legal fees and 
costs incurred by the Franchisor, arising out of the establishment and operation of the Franchised 
Business by the Franchisee. 
 
The Franchisor may in its discretion, acting reasonably, designate in writing from time to time the Items 
that are to be purchased by the Franchisee, either from the Franchisor or from a supplier specified by 
the Franchisor.  The Franchisee acknowledges that this requirement is reasonable in order to have 
consistency and effective quality control within the Franchise System, and that consistency and 
effective quality control is essential for the success of the Franchised Business and the Franchise System.  
The Franchisor will take reasonable steps to ensure that the prices of such items are in line with 
comparable items from other suppliers, after taking into account the quality of the goods and any 
additional costs which may be occasioned by the labelling or packaging of the items specifically for 
the purposes of the Business System.  If the Franchisee wishes to pursue an opportunity to acquire the 
Items, which are not branded or specific to the Franchised Business, from different suppliers, whether 
at a lower price or not, then the Franchisee hereby undertakes to notify the Franchisor in writing.  The 
Franchisor will then have a period of 21 (twenty one) business days to investigate the quality of the 
proposed items from the alternative supplier, at the cost of the Franchisee.  The Franchisee undertakes 
to reimburse the Franchisor for all reasonable expenses incurred in this regard on demand.  If the 
quality of the item is, in the sole discretion of the Franchisor, found to be identical or better than the 
quality of the same item from the designated supplier, and if the item is in all other respects identical 
to or better than the same item from the designated supplier, then the Franchisor will have a further 
14 (fourteen) days to attempt to negotiate a similar, competitive price from the designated supplier, 
failing which the Franchisee will be entitled to source the item from the alternative supplier for the time 
being.  The Parties hereby specifically agree and record that the Franchisor will have the right to insist 
and direct that the Franchisee sources the item from the approved supplier if the alternative supplier’s 
item does not conform to or exceed the quality and other standards applicable to the Franchise 
System, both at the time of consenting to the use of an alternative supplier and thereafter on an 
ongoing basis.  A supplier database shall be provided to the Franchisee by the Franchisor along with 
order procedures as set out in the Operations Manual.  The Franchisee undertakes to provide for 
7 (seven) working days, subsequent to placing the order, in order to allow for items to be delivered by

the Franchisor.  All items ordered will be paid by electronic payment together with Franchise Fee.  All 
other orders will require timeous settlement directly with the various suppliers. 
 
The Franchisee will give notice, in the form acceptable to and stipulated by the Franchisor from time 
to time, in such places on the Premises as the Franchisor may from time to time require, that the 
Franchised Business is operated in terms of a franchise agreement with the Franchisor and that the 
Franchisee is using the Intellectual Property under licence from the Franchisor.  Such notice will contain 
such other information as the Franchisor may deem necessary to inform third parties that the Franchisor 
does not accept any liability for the acts, omissions, debts and/or default of the Franchisee. The 
Franchisee will use the term “individually owned and operated” in referring to Bootlegger Coffee 
Company to indicate its independent operation of the Franchisor. 
 
The Franchisee will, at its own cost, maintain such polices of insurance as may be reasonably stipulated 
by the Franchisor from time to time and will ensure that all insurance premiums are paid promptly.  The 
Franchisee will ensure that the interest of the Franchisor in such policies is noted thereon.  The 
Franchisee will ensure that a note is made on all policies maintained by it in accordance with the 
provisions of this Agreement that the insurer will notify the Franchisor in the event of the late payment 
of any premium by the Franchisee.  The Franchisee will furnish the Franchisor with all receipts for the 
payment of premiums on request.  The Franchisee will not cause or permit any breach of any insurance 
policy maintained under the provisions of this Agreement.  The Franchisee will give the Franchisor 
notice of any claim arising or made under a policy of insurance required to be maintained in terms of 
this Agreement. Such policies of insurance are to be in place prior to the Commencement Date and 
the Franchisee undertakes to ensure that the Franchisor is furnished with a copy of such insurance 
policies prior to the Commencement Date. 
 
The Franchisee indemnifies and holds the Franchisor harmless against any claims arising from the 
operation of the Business System by the Franchisee. 
 
The Franchisee will at all reasonable times and in accordance with the Operations Manual, allow the 
Franchisor full and immediate access to all documentation and financial information relating to the 
Franchised Business, including, without limitation, contracts, bank statements, invoices, point of sale 
printouts, credit card slips and receipts.  The Franchisee will, at its own cost, maintain, in a form 
approved by the Franchisor (and preserve same for at least 5 (five) years after termination of this 
Agreement), full and accurate books of account and all underlying or supporting records and 
vouchers relating to the Franchised Business and permit the Franchisor and/or the Franchisor’s auditors, 
at any time, to inspect and/or audit the said accounts and records and take copies thereof.  The 
Franchisee shall also, at its own cost, provide to the Franchisor, annually, comprehensive financial 
reports prepared by a suitably qualified accountant, including projected cash flows and full details of 
total Turnover and profits and/or losses, prepared by a chartered accountant in accordance with 
generally accepted accounting practice and in accordance with the Franchisor’s requirements from 
time to time.  
 
The Franchisee shall act strictly in accordance with the Franchisor's instructions and directions 
regarding the running of the Franchised Business. 
 
The Franchisee shall refer any and all queries it may receive regarding the Franchised Business, the 
Business System, the Franchise System or regarding becoming a Franchisee to the Franchisor,

immediately upon receiving such queries, and without expressing any opinion thereon to the person 
from whom they emanate. 
 
The Franchisee shall at all times maintain acceptable standards of cleanliness, tidiness, orderliness, 
professionalism, fairness and ethical conduct throughout the term of this Agreement.  This also includes 
ensuring that the name, reputation and good standing of the Franchised Business and the Franchise 
System is promoted and never impaired in any way.  In addition, the Franchisee shall not do anything 
or refrain from taking any action that might reasonably be expected to result in harming the Goodwill, 
validity of or value associated with the Intellectual Property, the Business System and/or the Franchise 
System. 
 
Without being entitled to any remuneration therefore, the Franchisee agrees and undertakes that any 
and all conceptions, inventions, confidential information, procedures, technical information, 
techniques and other Intellectual Property, which it may receive by way of assignment, invent, 
discover, or work out during the term of this Agreement (whether during regular working hours or not) 
which relate to or could be used in connection with the activities of the Franchisor, shall be for the

in the future as and when the Franchisor so requires.  The Franchisee hereby undertakes, when called 
upon to do so by the Franchisor, to, without charge, sign all documentation and do any and all things 
necessary to obtain and/or record such Intellectual Property and/or the assignment thereof to the 
Franchisor. 
 
The Franchisee undertakes to always use its full legal name, followed by the trading name in all 
correspondence and/or dealings with third parties relating to or associated with the Franchised 
Business.  The aforementioned will specifically include, without limitation, the name identifying the 
Franchisee’s bank accounts and cheques used in relation to or associated with the Franchised 
Business.  
 
The Parties agree that the point of sale system, computer, printer, accounting and related systems 
which are to be acquired in terms the Operating Manual form part of the Business System.  The 
Franchisee therefore undertakes to strictly comply with the Franchisor’s directions regarding the 
operating of these systems as reflected in the Operations Manual from time to time.   If the Franchisor 
in its sole discretion decides to effect improvements or additions to these systems, including the 
addition or updating of an on-line, dial-up or other computer based or similar link with the Franchisee, 
the Franchisee will grant unrestricted and uninterrupted access to its accounting system or any other 
system that is utilised by the Franchisee in conducting the Franchised Business.  Nothing contained in 
this clause shall be construed as placing any obligation on the Franchisor to install such a link, and if 
such a link is provided by the Franchisor at some stage, the link and all related systems and software 
will be deemed to form part of the Intellectual Property licensed in terms of this Agreement.  The 
Franchisee acknowledges that the database details of clients remain the property of the Franchisor 
which the Franchisee will provide to the Franchisor for electronic mailings of marketing material and 
newsletters. 
 
The Franchisee will be obliged, at his own expense, to attend all meetings called by the Franchisor, at 
such reasonable venues and times as the Franchisor may from time to time stipulate.

All promotional and advertising material must be approved by the Franchisor.   
 
The Franchisee is obligated to participate in national advertising (via the Marketing Fee) and regional 
marketing initiatives and promotions, from time to time, as and when necessary. 
 
The Franchisee is obligated to join in and comply with any group sales and marketing promotions, 
loyalty programmes, discounts schemes  or any such marketing incentives that the Franchisor 
reasonably deems to be to the benefit and success of the Franchised Business. The Franchisee will be 
responsible for the reasonable cost of in-store marketing material. 
 
The Franchisee undertakes to conduct local marketing initiatives, provided that prior to 
commencement of such marketing initiatives the Franchisor consents to such initiatives, in writing, 
which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld. 
 
The trading hours of the Franchised Business shall be agreed to with the Franchisor prior to the 
Commencement Date.  Any amendments to trading hours are to be consented to, in writing, by the 
Franchisor. 
 
The Franchisee undertakes to repair any equipment required to facilitate the running of Franchised 
Business within 24 (twenty four) hours of becoming aware of the problem.  In the event that such 
equipment cannot be repaired within 24 (twenty four) hours, the Franchisee undertakes to source 
back-up equipment required for the running of the Franchised Business immediately, whether from a 
supplier or from another franchised business within the Franchise System.  
 
The Franchisee undertakes to ensure that repairs and maintenance to the Premises takes place within 
48 (forty eight) hours of it becoming aware of the problem. 
 
The employment of the members of staff of the Franchised Business shall be the responsibility of the 
Franchisee.  The Franchisor will provide the Franchisee with a staffing structure and wage rate 
recommendation document and the Franchisee undertakes to pay members of its staff no less than 
the wage rate proposed in the wage rate recommendation document. 
 
The Franchisee undertakes to make available for inspection by the Franchisor all contracts of 
employment, disciplinary notices, copies of employees’ Identity Documents, and sick notes within 24 
(twenty four) hours of receipt of written notice from the Franchisor.  The Franchisee undertakes to keep 
securely all such documents on the Premises. 
 
The Franchisee undertakes to provide a tax clearance certificate to the Franchisor prior to the 
Commencement Date. 
 
The Franchisee undertakes to apply for registration as a VAT vendor prior to the Commencement Date 
and shall provide to the Franchisor of such registration.  
 
The Franchisee undertakes to provide the Franchisor with a copy of its corporate registration 
documentation (if applicable) prior to the Commencement Date. 
 
The Franchisee undertakes to complete any property upgrades and improvements to the Premises, 
which forms part of the Business System’s planned programme to redecorate the front of house area

of the Franchised Business, which is scheduled to take place every 5 (five) years.  The extent of such

If the Franchisee is a company or close corporation, the Nominated Operator shall hold no less than 
10% (ten percent) of the share capital or members interest as the case may be and shall be personally 
present and actively involved in the running of the business on a day to day basis, and thus personally 
remain in effective control of the business at all times, and shall not be absent from the business for 
more than 2 (two) consecutive days unless such absence is due to illness or regular vacation, which 
vacation shall not exceed 21 (twenty) one days in any 12 (twelve) month period.  
 
The Nominated Operator shall not permit any person other than a properly trained manager to 
manage and control the business without the prior written consent of the Franchisor. 
13. 
TRADE MARKS 
 
The Franchisee will not in any way represent that it has any rights of any nature in the Intellectual 
Property including the Trade Marks and all use of the Trade Marks and Intellectual Property will inure 
to the benefit of the Franchisor. 
 
Subject to clause 12.29, the Franchisee will not register or use any of the Intellectual Property including 
the Trade Marks, or any part thereof, or any trade mark which is deceptively or confusingly similar to 
the Trade Marks as part of its company, close corporation or trading name or the name of any 
subsidiary entity or other entity with which it is associated either directly or indirectly and if it does so, it 
will procure that such name is changed upon demand by the Franchisor, failing which the Franchisor 
is hereby authorised to do so. 
 
When using the Trade Marks, the Franchisee will cause them to be reproduced exactly and accurately 
and in accordance with specifications and directions laid down by the Franchisor from time to time.   
 
Upon termination of this Agreement for whatever reason: 
 
the Franchisee will immediately cease any further use of the Intellectual Property, specifically 
including the Trade Marks; 
 
the Franchisee will on demand hand over to the Franchisor all advertising matter, printed matter, 
the Operations Manual and material or equipment, featuring or embodying the Trade Marks or 
Intellectual Property which were obtained from or which the Franchisee was authorised to use by 
the Franchisor (including fixtures and fittings) and shall remove all signage and branding with 
immediate effect.  The Franchisor shall pay the Franchisee the cost price of such items subject to 
the condition of such items; 
 
The Franchisee shall not disparage nor do anything calculated to disparage or damage, nor refrain 
from doing anything which has the effect of disparaging or damaging the Franchisor’s Goodwill, 
reputation or Intellectual Property.

14. 
OPERATIONS MANUAL 
 
The Franchisor will, at least 30 (thirty) days prior to the Opening Date, provide the Franchisee with the 
current Operations Manual, and will provide the Franchisee from time to time with amendments, 
variations and updates in respect of the Operations Manual. 
 
All of the provisions of the Operations Manual, any new edition of the Operations Manual, and any 
amendments to or revisions, variations and/or updates of the Operations Manual form part of this 
Agreement save that in the event of any conflict between the terms of this Agreement and provisions 
in the Operations Manual, this Agreement will prevail. 
 
The Franchisee will not make any unauthorised copies of the Operations Manual and upon termination 
of this Agreement will return all copies of the Operations Manual to the Franchisor. 
 
The Operations Manual and any similar or associated documentation shall: 
 
at all times be and remain the sole and exclusive property of the Franchisor; 
 
be kept by the Franchisee in a safe place approved by the Franchisor and inaccessible to anyone 
other than the Franchisee; 
 
be regularly and promptly updated by the Franchisee by the insertion therein as directed of such 
updating material as is supplied from time to time by the Franchisor; 
 
not be displayed or disclosed or made available by the Franchisee to any other person without the 
express prior written consent of the Franchisor and then only in strict  compliance with the 
Franchisor’s directions with regard thereto; 
 
not, for any reason whatsoever, be copied, loaned, sold, hired out or in any manner circulated or 
distributed or disseminated without the express prior written consent thereto of the Franchisor and 
then only in strict compliance with the Franchisor’s directions with regard thereto; 
 
be maintained in good order and condition; 
 
be regarded as having been amended, varied or updated upon delivery by the Franchisor to the 
Franchisee of written details of such amendments, variations or updates; 
 
be replaced by the Franchisor at the cost of the Franchisee in the event of damage thereto or loss 
thereof.  
 
In the event that a dispute arises between the Parties hereto as to what constitutes the correct 
contents of the Operations Manual, the master copy thereof retained by the Franchisor shall be 
regarded as containing the correct and current contents thereof. 
15. 
NO CESSION, ASSIGNMENT, SALE OR LEASE 
 
The rights and obligations of the Franchisee are personal and may not be ceded, assigned, or 
otherwise encumbered or disposed of in any manner whatsoever without the prior written consent of 
the Franchisor, which shall not be unreasonably withheld, nor may the Franchisee at any stage appoint 
any sub-Franchisee without prior written consent.  If the Franchisee is a close corporation, company, 
DocuSign Envelope ID: B91ADC09-CA91-4454-ADB0-22E6783FF674


<!-- PAGE 23 START -->
 
 
 
page | 22 
Bootlegger_Franchise Agreement_2020_v2.1.docx 
partnership or other legal entity no change of membership, shareholding, directors, partners or office 
bearers may take place without the prior written consent of the Franchisor. 
 
In particular the Franchisee may not sell or assign any right whatsoever in or to the Franchised Business 
without the prior written consent of the Franchisor.  The Franchisor may in its sole discretion give consent 
to the sale of the Franchised Business provided the prospective purchaser meets its Franchisee criteria 
and the following conditions: 
 
that the purchase price relating to the Franchised Business is reasonable, taking into account the 
prevailing market conditions as at the date of the offer; 
 
that the Franchisee has satisfied all its financial and other obligations in terms of the Agreement to 
the Franchisor; 
 
that the Franchisee furnishes the Franchisor with a copy of the proposed agreement of sale or 
disposal; and 
 
that the potential purchaser is approved by the Franchisor and the potential purchaser concludes 
and signs the Franchisor’s standard franchise agreement at the time, save that the Upfront License 
Fee currently stipulated in clause 5 (or its equivalent in the standard agreement at that time) will 
be payable as to 100% (hundred percent) of the current Upfront License Fee. 
 
Any proposed sale or transfer of the Franchised Business or any part thereof shall be subject to the 
Franchisor’s right of first refusal.  The Franchisor’s right of first refusal shall be exercised in the following 
manner: 
 
the Franchisee shall forthwith give written notice to the Franchisor of any bona fide offer received 
by the Franchisee from any third party; 
 
the Franchisee shall be obliged to first offer to sell or dispose of the Franchised Business to the 
Franchisor upon the same terms and conditions and for the same consideration (expressed in South 
African rand) as stipulated in the bona fide offer; 
 
the Franchisee shall be obliged to make available for inspection, by the Franchisor all accounts, all 
staff contracts, leases and any other documents deemed appropriate by the Franchisor; 
 
the Franchisor shall have 21 (twenty one) business days after receipt of such notice within which to 
accept the offer in question.  The Parties also specifically agree that the Franchisor may cede his 
right of first refusal to his nominee, who will be entitled to exercise the option and accept the offer 
in its own name; 
 
should the Franchisor or the nominee elect not to accept the offer, then the Franchisee shall, 
subject to clause 15.2, be permitted to sell or dispose of the Franchised Business upon exactly the 
same terms and conditions and for exactly the same consideration as stipulated in the offer, and 
on no terms or conditions and for no consideration more favourable to the third party than the 
offer from the Franchisee to the Franchisor, subject to clause 15.6. 
 
The Franchisee will not advertise and/or publish the fact that it is looking for a potential buyer in any 
manner whatsoever, without the prior written consent of the Franchisor and the Franchisee specifically 
DocuSign Envelope ID: B91ADC09-CA91-4454-ADB0-22E6783FF674

page | 23 
Bootlegger_Franchise Agreement_2020_v2.1.docx 
agree that the Franchisor may require compliance with reasonable restrictions relating to the method 
and/or style of advertising and/or publication before granting consent, and that this restriction is 
reasonable and necessary in order to protect the Business System, the Franchised Business and/or the 
Franchise System.  The Franchisee will not disclose any Intellectual Property of a confidential nature to 
a prospective purchaser without the prior written consent of the Franchisor, which consent will not be 
unreasonably withheld.  The Parties specifically agree and record that the Franchisor may require the 
prospective purchaser to sign a confidentiality agreement, in a form acceptable to the Franchisor, 
before the disclosure of any information. 
 
The Franchisor may, in its discretion, cede, delegate and assign any of its obligations and rights under 
this Agreement.  
 
If Franchisor declines to exercise its options in terms of clause 15.3, the Franchisee shall not assign or 
otherwise transfer this Agreement, the  Franchised Business (and  any  interest  therein) or the 
Franchisee’s interest without prior written approval of the Franchisor. Any sale, session, assignment or 
disposal which takes place with Franchisor’s approval shall be conditional upon and subject to: 
 
the Franchisee paying the Franchisor for advising, reviewing and assist in in the assignment or 
transfer of this Agreement, or of the Franchised Business (or any interest therein) or the Franchisee 
(and any interest therein), or any sale, cession, assignment or disposal thereof, the Franchisee 
paying to the Franchisor a transfer fee equivalent to 5% of the total sales price of the Franchised

the Purchaser or Transferee being suitably qualified in terms of the Franchisor’s then prevailing 
selection and recruitment criteria and paying to Franchisor the Upfront License Fee in the amount 
chargeable by Franchisor to new Franchisees at such time;  and 
 
the new Franchisee signing the then current Franchise Agreement. 
16. 
DEATH OF THE FRANCHISEE 
 
If the Franchisee is a natural person, then on the death of the Franchisee the Franchisor may approve 
the transfer of the Franchised Business to any of the beneficiaries of the deceased Franchisee.  Such 
person(s) will be required to assume the management of the Franchised Business as soon as is 
practicable once they have bound themselves to observe the terms and conditions of this Agreement. 
Pending such transfer, the Franchisor will be entitled to assume the control and management of the 
Franchised Business and to operate it on behalf of the representative of the deceased estate of the 
Franchisee, and for the benefit and account of the deceased estate of the Franchisee provided that: 
 
the Franchisor will be entitled to charge its usual fee for the conduct and management of the 
Franchised Business, and the Franchisor is hereby authorised to do all things and to sign all 
documents reasonably necessary for the purposes of conducting and managing the Franchised 
Business; and 
 
if the Franchisor does not approve the transfer of the Franchised Business to a beneficiary in terms 
of clause 16.1, the representative of the deceased estate of the Franchisee may dispose of the 
Franchised Business to such person as may be approved by the Franchisor, for such sale price as

page | 24

may be agreed upon between the person concerned and the representative of the deceased 
estate of the Franchisee; and 
 
the purchaser of the Franchised Business may not take transfer thereof until he has bound himself 
to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, and pending such sale and transfer, the Franchisor 
will be entitled to operate and manage the Franchised Business on behalf of and for the account 
of the estate of the deceased Franchisee.  The Franchisor will be entitled to charge its usual fee for 
managing and conducting the Franchised Business and will be authorised to do all things and to 
sign all documents reasonably necessary to enable it to conduct and manage the Franchised 
Business; and 
 
if a suitable beneficiary and/or purchaser cannot be found within a reasonable time after the 
death of the Franchisee, the Franchisor shall have the right to terminate this Agreement on 30 
(thirty) days’ written notice.  The suitability of the beneficiary and/or purchaser shall be within the 
sole discretion of the Franchisor, it being understood that the Franchisor will exercise his discretion 
in a reasonable manner and with view to maintaining and if possible enhancing the standards of 
the Franchise System; 
 
if the Franchisee is a close corporation, a company or a partnership, then, upon the death of a

approval of the Franchisor upon such terms and conditions for approval as the Franchisor may 
determine, be transferred to the remaining members or shareholders or partners or the 
beneficiaries of the deceased person.  Failing such transfer, the interest or shares of the deceased 
person may be sold or transferred to such person or persons as may be approved of by the 
Franchisor upon such terms and conditions as may be agreed upon between the transferee and 
the representative of the estate of the deceased person.  The Franchisor may, if it deems it 
expedient, and pending the transfer of the interest or share of the deceased person, participate 
in the control and management of the Franchised Business and will be entitled to charge its usual 
fee for such participation, payable by the Franchisee.  The Franchisor will be authorised to do all 
such things and to sign all such documents reasonably necessary for the purposes of participating 
in such control and management of the Franchised Business.  If a suitable beneficiary and/or 
purchaser cannot be found within a reasonable time after the death of the member or shareholder 
or partner, the Franchisor shall have the right to terminate this Agreement on 30 (thirty) days’ written 
notice.  The suitability of the beneficiary and/or purchaser shall be within the sole discretion of the 
Franchisor, it being understood that the Franchisor will exercise his discretion in a reasonable 
manner and with view to maintaining and if possible enhancing the standards of the Franchise 
System. 
 
At the Commencement Date and thereafter from time to time, the Parties will determine key staff 
members (including the Franchisee if he is a natural person, and office bearers, employees, partners, 
directors, members or shareholders of the Franchisee as the case may be).  In the event of the death, 
incapacity, failure to act, resignation or dismissal of a key staff member, the Franchisor may itself 
provide a person, which in its discretion it considers suitable, to fulfil the key position vacated by such 
key staff member until the Franchisee is able to fill the key position to the satisfaction of the Franchisor.  
The person provided by the Franchisor will be deemed to be an employee of the Franchisee, and the

17. 
TERMINATION OF THIS AGREEMENT 
 
The Franchisor on written notice to the Franchisee shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement should 
it fail to pay, by the due date, any sums due and owing hereunder and failing so to pay within 7 (seven) 
days of written notice, calling upon that Party to make payment.  
 
The Franchisor will be entitled to terminate on written notice to the Franchisee if the Franchisee: 
 
in any other way breaches the terms and conditions of this Agreement (all of the Franchisee’s 
obligations under this Agreement are declared to be material to this Agreement) and failing so to 
remedy the breach within 7 (seven) days of written notice by or on behalf of the Franchisor, calling 
upon the Franchisee to remedy the breach complained of; 
 
commits an act of insolvency or is sequestrated, provisionally or finally, or is placed under a 
provisional or final winding-up or judicial management order or if the Franchisee makes an 
assignment for the benefit of any of its creditors, or if the Franchisee attempts to enter into any 
compromise with any of its creditors, or becomes incapacitated in any way including, but not 
limited to, being placed under curatorship by reason of ill health (mental or otherwise), or fails to 
satisfy or take steps to have set aside any judgment taken against the Franchisee, within 21 (twenty 
one) days after such judgment has come to the notice of the Franchisee; 
 
if any change occurs in the management, ownership or control of the Franchised Business or of the 
Franchisee otherwise than in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement; 
 
attacks or challenges the rights of the Franchisor to the Intellectual Property or induces or procures 
any other person to make such challenge or attack; 
 
performance, competence or standard of services are poor to the extent that contracts and/or 
clients and/or potential Franchisees and/or potential Franchised Businesses are lost or at risk to be 
lost which is evidenced by the Franchisee failing to meet the Franchisee’s written performance 
criteria; 
 
fails to act in accordance with directives and instructions (as set out in, inter alia, the Operations 
Manual) made in the best interests of the Franchise System and/or Business System from time to 
time; 
 
abandons or fails to actively operate the Franchised Business; or 
 
has made any material misrepresentation or omission to the Franchisor; or

abandons  or  in any other  manner  relinquishes  an  ownership  interest or  any part thereof in the

is a party to any other franchise agreement with Franchisor that is terminated by the Franchisor for

withheld; or 
 
commits the same breach more than twice in any 12 (twelve) month period and fails to remedy 
such breach within 7 (seven) days of written notice by or on behalf of the Franchisor, calling upon 
the Franchisee to remedy the breach complained of; or 
 
the Nominated Operator is absent from the Franchised Business for more than 2 (two) consecutive 
days or within 21 (twenty one) leave days per year, excluding leave for medical reasons.

The terminating of this Agreement, for whatever reason, will not affect the rights of a Party which may 
have accrued as at the date of termination, and will further not affect any rights and obligations which 
specifically or by their nature survive the termination of this Agreement. 
 
In the event of the termination of this Agreement for whatever reason, the Franchisee will not dispose 
of any item bearing the Trade Marks without prior written consent of the Franchisor and such consent 
may be given on such conditions as the Franchisor may deem fit, including the condition that the 
Trade Marks be removed or obliterated from the item concerned. 
 
Upon termination the Franchisor shall not pay any goodwill relating to the premises or area to the 
Franchisee and all goodwill relating to the Intellectual Property and the Franchised Business shall 
accrue to the Franchisor. 
 
At the termination of this Agreement for any reason whatsoever, the Franchisee shall, if it remains in 
occupation of the Premises, forthwith change the appearance of the Premises both inside and 
outside, at its own cost, to the extent reasonably directed by Franchisor, to prevent the Premises being 
mistaken in appearance by members of the public for a Franchised Business or having any link with a 
Franchised Business. 
 
If this Agreement is terminated by the Franchisee prior to expiration of this Agreement by effluxion of 
time or by Franchisor due to a default of Franchisee, either in regard to its obligations to the lessor of 
the Premises or to Franchisor, then the Franchisee shall be obliged to pay Franchisor damages for the 
unexpired portion of the franchise period, calculated in accordance with the following formula - 
X  x (Y ÷ Z) 
where X = the royalties already paid and payable to date of termination, and where Y = the number 
of months expired in terms of this Agreement, and 
where Z = the number of unexpired months of this Agreement. 
 
Provided that, should this Agreement be terminated by mutual written consent between the parties, 
then this clause  17.7 shall have no application and there shall be no liability on the Franchisee to pay 
any damages to the Franchisor as set out above. 
 
Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, the Franchisor shall be  entitled  
to  claim  from  the Franchisee  such  additional  or  alternative  damages  as the Franchisor may have 
suffered as a result of the Franchisee's breach of this Agreement and the cancellation thereof and the 
Franchisee shall bear all legal costs (inclusive of costs as between an attorney and his own client) and 
such accounting costs as the Franchisor may have incurred pursuant to any breach of the Agreement 
18. 
RESTRAINT OF TRADE 
 
While this Agreement is in force and for a period of 12 (twelve) months after termination for any reason, 
neither the Franchisee nor any of its Associates will, within 10 kilometres of any Bootleggers site, directly 
or indirectly, in any manner whatsoever (whether alone or jointly or together with or as an agent for 
any other person, partnership, company, body corporate, association, business or undertaking of any 
nature whatsoever):

Notwithstanding clauses 18.1 to 18.4 inclusive, the Franchisee and/or its Associates may, during the

that, prior to doing so, the Franchisee shall have requested written permission to do so from the 
Franchisor and provided further that the Franchisor has granted such permission in writing, which 
permission shall not be unreasonably withheld.  
 
For the sake of clarity, the Franchisor shall not be obligated to grant permission to the Franchisee in 
instances when the proposed business replicates or is in direct competition with the Franchised 
Business.

Agreement.  The Franchisee specifically confirms the matters reflected in the disclosure document have 
been disclosed prior to the conclusion of the Agreement.

22. 
STATUTORY COMPLIANCE 
 
In terms of the Act: 
 
the Franchisor undertakes to disclose and explain to the Franchisee any direct or indirect benefits 
or compensation it recovers from suppliers to the Franchisees; 
 
the Franchisor shall provide the Franchisee with a written explanation of any terms or sections not 
fully understood by the Franchisee upon request in writing by the Franchisee; 
 
the Agreement shall not allow unreasonable or overvaluation of fees, prices or other direct or 
indirect consideration; 
 
the Agreement shall not provide for conduct which is unreasonable or unnecessary in relation to 
the risks incurred or in the interests of the Franchise System.

24. 
NOTICES 
Every notice, consent or other communication required or permitted in terms of this Agreement from 
either Party shall be in writing. It shall be sufficiently given or transmitted if and when: 
 
hand-delivered to the other Party at its address, or at such other address as the Party may have 
designated in writing; 
 
sent by e-mail to the specified e-mail address, provided that no indication that it has not been 
delivered is received by the sender within a reasonable time after dispatch; or 
 
deposited in the mail, duly registered with postage prepaid for prompt delivery and addressed to the 
other Party at its address, or at such other address as the addressee may have designated in writing.  
A notice deposited in the mail in terms of this sub-clause shall be deemed to have been delivered on 
the 7th (seventh) business day after the date of posting. 
25. 
GENERAL 
 
No waiver by a Party of any breach, failure or default in performance by the other Party, and no 
failure, refusal or neglect by a Party to exercise any right hereunder or to insist upon strict compliance 
with or performance of the other Party's obligations under this Agreement, shall constitute a waiver of 
the provisions of this Agreement and a Party may at any time require strict compliance with the 
provisions of this Agreement. 
 
This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties who acknowledge that there 
are no other oral or written understandings or agreements between them relating to the Products of 
this Agreement.  No amendment, cancellation or other modification of this Agreement shall be valid 
or binding on a Party hereto unless reduced to writing and executed by both Parties hereto. 
 
This Agreement shall be governed by and in accordance with the laws of South Africa. 
 
All the provisions of this Agreement shall be severable and no provision shall be affected by the 
invalidity of any other provision of this Agreement.  If any part or portion of this Agreement has been 
deemed to have been struck out and/or be declared a prohibited practice or the like in terms of the 
competition act or equivalent legislation, the effect of which is to adversely affect the rights of the 
Franchisor to receive payment of any nature or, in the Franchisor’s sole opinion, adversely affect the 
Intellectual Property, Business System, Franchised Business or Franchise System, either of the Parties may 
call on the other to within 7 (seven) days enter into good faith negotiations in an attempt to agree to 
an amendment of this Agreement or a new agreement which will put the Parties substantially in the 
same position as they were before.  In the event that the Parties cannot reach such agreement within 
21 (twenty one) days from entering into negotiations as contemplated, the Franchisor shall have the 
right to terminate this Agreement on 30 (thirty) days’ written notice to the Franchisee. 
 
Whenever a personal pronoun is used in this Agreement, it is understood that such usage shall include 
both singular and plural, masculine, feminine and neuter and refer in appropriate cases to juristic 
persons as well as natural persons. 
 
The Franchisee acknowledges that he has entered into this Agreement after making an independent 
investigation and that the Franchisor has not made any representations or warranties other than as
